task_description: Write a method in Dafny to solve the Dutch National Flag problem: sort an array of Red, White, and Blue elements.

primary_error.message: a postcondition could not be proved on this return path
primary_error.context: ensures forall i, j :: 0 <= i < j < a.Length ==> Below(a[i], a[j])

repair_strategy.problem_summary: Missing loop invariants cause index out of range errors and prevent postconditions from being verified for the Dutch Flag algorithm.

problem_details.what: The while loop lacks invariants to describe: (1) the relationship between pointers r, w, b and array bounds, (2) the color partitioning property of the array regions, and (3) the preservation of array elements. This causes 'index out of range' errors when accessing a[w], a[r], a[b-1] and prevents proving the sorting postcondition.
problem_details.why: The Dutch Flag algorithm partitions the array into four regions: [0,r) contains Red, [r,w) contains White, [w,b) is unprocessed, and [b,Length) contains Blue. Without invariants describing these regions, Dafny cannot verify that: (1) array accesses are within bounds (needs 0 <= r <= w <= b <= a.Length), (2) the final array is sorted (needs region color invariants), and (3) no elements are lost or created (needs multiset preservation invariant).
problem_details.how: Add five loop invariants: (1) 'invariant 0 <= r <= w <= b <= a.Length' to establish pointer ordering and bounds, ensuring all array accesses are safe; (2) 'invariant forall i :: 0 <= i < r ==> a[i] == Red' to track the red region; (3) 'invariant forall i :: r <= i < w ==> a[i] == White' to track the white region; (4) 'invariant forall i :: b <= i < a.Length ==> a[i] == Blue' to track the blue region; (5) 'invariant multiset(a[..]) == multiset(old(a[..]))' to ensure elements are only rearranged, not modified. When w == b, the unprocessed region is empty, and the color invariants directly imply the sorting postcondition.
